{"step_by_step_thinking": "g-H2AX (gamma-H2AX) is a well-established biomarker for DNA double-strand breaks. When double-strand breaks occur, the histone variant H2AX is rapidly phosphorylated at serine 139 to form gamma-H2AX. This phosphorylation serves as an early signal that recruits DNA repair proteins to the site of the break, making gamma-H2AX a key indicator of double-strand DNA damage.
